title: ARTIS Seafood Trade Matrix — Species-Disaggregated Bilateral Trade (Midpoint Estimates), 1996–2020
abstract: Global, species-disaggregated bilateral seafood trade flows reconstructed from UN Comtrade HS-code trade statistics by the ARTIS (Aquatic Resource Trade in Species) project. Records product weight and live-weight-equivalent trade between exporting, importing, and ultimate source countries, by scientific name, habitat, and production method (capture/aquaculture), across five Harmonized System classification revisions (HS96, HS02, HS07, HS12, HS17) spanning 1996-2020.

spatial.resolution: national (global bilateral trade matrix; not spatially disaggregated below country level)

resources.name: ARTIS bilateral trade, midpoint estimates (ZIP of 71 CSVs)

variables.name: hs6
variables.label: HS6 Product Code
variables.description: 6-digit Harmonized System product code for the traded item.

variables.name: dom_source
variables.label: Domestic/Foreign Source
variables.description: Whether the traded product originated domestically or was re-exported from a foreign source.

variables.name: source_country_iso3c
variables.label: Ultimate Source Country
variables.description: Country of ultimate production/catch origin for the traded product.

variables.name: sciname
variables.label: Scientific Name
variables.description: Taxon (species, genus, family, etc., depending on HS-code resolution) resolved for the traded product; see attribute tables for taxonomic hierarchy.

variables.name: habitat
variables.label: Habitat
variables.description: Marine or inland habitat of the source species.

variables.name: live_weight_t
variables.label: Live Weight Equivalent
variables.description: Product weight converted to live-weight equivalent.

variables.name: hs_version
variables.label: HS Classification Version
variables.description: Harmonized System revision used for this record's coding (HS96, HS02, HS07, HS12, or HS17).

access.how_to_request: ARTIS is a research-consortium data product understood to be openly published; exact access portal/terms were not verified locally. Local ZIP copy available on request from the KAUST Data Inventory Team pending confirmation of redistribution terms.

provenance.originator: ARTIS (Aquatic Resource Trade in Species) research consortium

known_issues: The ZIP contains 71 separate CSVs, one per HS-classification/year combination (HS96: 1996-2020, HS02: 2002-2020, HS07: 2007-2020, HS12: 2012-2020, HS17: 2017-2020). Coverage years overlap by design across HS revisions (ARTIS reprocesses recent years under multiple HS systems) — naive concatenation of all files will duplicate trade flows; a single HS version per year must be selected before aggregating.
n_rows (~90 million) is an order-of-magnitude estimate derived from total uncompressed size (~11.8 GB) and average observed row length; it was not computed exactly because of file size.

Global dataset, not filtered to Saudi Arabia or the Red Sea; must be filtered on importer_iso3c / exporter_iso3c / source_country_iso3c = 'SAU' for Kingdom-relevant subsets. Even filtered to Saudi Arabia, the data cannot distinguish Red Sea vs. Arabian Gulf origin/destination.
Requires joining with the 'artis-attribute-tables' source (attribute_tables.zip) for full taxonomic resolution, product descriptions, and country groupings.

Originator was identified from internal filenames (artis_midpoint_*) and the archive's own attributes_README, which references 'ARTIS data'; the exact publication, license, and DOI were not independently verified and should be confirmed before external use or redistribution.
ZIP also contains macOS resource-fork artifacts (__MACOSX/ entries) alongside the 71 real data files; these should be ignored when extracting.
